summary: Fire‑filled Other One, stellar China>Rider, rare Foolish Heart encore
blurb: The second set explodes with a monster The Other One and a tight China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition. Jerry’s lead work is on fire while Brent adds bright keys throughout. The rare Foolish Heart encore caps an energetic night.
review: On August 19, 1989 the Grateful Dead closed out their summer run at Berkeley's Greek Theatre with a performance that still feels fresh.  Set 1 delivered solid staples – a rollicking Let The Good Times Roll and a crisp Box of Rain – but it was the second set that stole the show.  A flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue launched into an extended Playing In The Band, leading to a double‑intro The Other One where Jerry Garcia ripped through his solos with searing intensity and Brent’s keys painted dissonant colors.  The band rode that momentum through Space, a soaring Wharf Rat, and a rousing Not Fade Away before bowing out with the seldom‑heard Foolish Heart as an encore, giving collectors a rare treat.  Across all sources the playing is described as “locked in” and “on fire,” making this one of the high‑water marks of the Dead’s late‑’80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across multiple recordings: numerous reviewers praise The Other One, China>Rider, Foolish Heart and overall band lock‑in; no consistent negative comments about performance quality.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality FM capture with clear tone, covers all highlights and avoids the dropouts found on other sources.

key_highlights: Monster The Other One jam with Jerry’s scorching lead work
Tight China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue that drives the second set
Rare Foolish Heart encore gives a unique closing touch
Overall band lock‑in and high energy throughout Set 2

band_performance.Jerry: On fire all night, especially during The Other One and the Rider solo; tone described as killer and ‘guns blazing’.
band_performance.Phil: Deep bass bombs and a double‑Phil intro on The Other One; provides solid drive throughout the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Adds vocal depth, joins Jerry on the final line of Rider and maintains strong stage presence.
band_performance.Brent: Keys add bright dissonant color to The Other One and generally great keyboard work; occasional weak moment noted but overall lifts the band.
band_performance.Mickey: Tight finger‑drumming, drives the Rider outro and fuels the psychedelic climax in Space.
band_performance.Billy: Extended drumming praised for its power and propulsion through the second‑set jams.
